title: Avoid duplicate checkout auth during release checks

sources.reason: Release distribution checks must avoid duplicate checkout auth when running Git commands in private CI.
Regression coverage must confirm the Git command isolation and checkout-auth reuse behavior.

riskAssessment.reason: The fix changes how release validation reuses GitHub auth context without changing public release semantics.

executionDecision.reason: The release auth reuse fix is concrete and verifiable.

preReviewWarnings: Verify that no credential material is echoed into logs.
Verify Git commands do not inherit duplicate checkout auth headers.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: make check-release-distribution completes in a private repository CI context without duplicate Authorization headers.
The release distribution check still works in a public repository environment.
Regression tests cover checkout-auth reuse and isolated Git command execution.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: GitHub Actions now runs the repository as private, and the release distribution check can fail when it reuses checkout auth twice during the public tag verification flow.
constraints: Do not change public release validation semantics beyond preventing duplicate auth injection.
Keep the check reproducible in CI and locally.
Do not expose credential material in logs.
intent.rationale: Run release Git commands from an isolated working directory so the checkout-auth context can be reused safely without being injected twice.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
